task_completion.description: Weasis application is open and the DICOM series is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis GUI is visible with a medical image (X-Ray Angiography) displayed in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: A multi-panel grid layout is configured in Weasis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis viewer area is split into a grid layout containing at least three separate panels.

task_completion.description: The same DICOM series is displayed across multiple panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The same anatomical image is visible in at least three of the grid panels.

task_completion.description: Distinctly different Window/Level settings are applied to at least three panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The images in at least three panels have noticeably different contrast and brightness levels, indicating unlinked display properties and distinct Window/Level adjustments.

task_completion.description: The multi-window setup screenshot is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or screenshot tool shows the image saved as 'trauma_layout.png' in the '~/DICOM/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: The WC and WW values are recorded in the required text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the file '~/DICOM/exports/window_levels.txt' containing the Window Center (WC) and Window Width (WW) values for at least three panels in the format 'Panel X: WC [value], WW [value]'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Weasis GUI to configure the layout and adjust window levels.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with Weasis menus, layout grid buttons, and window/level adjustment tools, rather than using external scripts to manipulate images.

integrity.description: The agent captured the screenshot from the actual Weasis application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a screenshot tool or Weasis's built-in export function to save the layout, rather than downloading or moving a pre-existing image.

integrity.description: The recorded WC and WW values correspond to the actual settings applied in Weasis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into the text file match the Window/Level values visible in the Weasis GUI overlays for each respective panel.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The DICOM sample is actually an X-Ray Angiography (XA) sample, despite the task description referring to it as a 'trauma CT'. The expected output files are '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/window_levels.txt' and '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/trauma_layout.png'. The agent must configure at least 3 distinct panels with unique Window Center (WC) and Window Width (WW) pairs.
